news.title: A 'perfect storm' points to a much smaller U.S. auto market by 2040
news.summary: One forecaster says this is a fundamental change, and it is going to get worse. The auto industry is selling fewer cars, a new report says.

news.title: Trump again threatens Iran with annihilation as Kuwait and Bahrain report attacks
news.summary: Iran says it targeted U.S. military facilities in Kuwait and Bahrain following U.s. strikes on targets in and around the Strait of Hormuz.

news.title: Seniors in Medicare are about to get landmark obesity drug coverage — but many may not know it yet
news.summary: Many seniors may be unaware of the landmark coverage shift starting on July 1, with limited advertising seen from the government or Eli Lilly and Novo.

news.title: The memory shortage shaking Apple and Microsoft is 'existential crisis' for smaller players
news.summary: Smaller consumer electronics companies are in dire straits. Apple and Microsoft raise prices on key devices to help cover the soaring costs of memory.

news.title: From protein coffee to CBD soda: How brands are cashing in on the functional beverage boom
news.summary: "We're selling [almost] as much protein cold foam as we do flat whites," Starbuck's EMEA Manager of Beverage Development Sam Henderson said.

news.title: Your health insurer shouldn’t decide your treatment plan. That’s what your doctor — and AI — should be doing.
news.summary: With a digital health record, clinicians and AI-driven diagnostic tools would be able to see your full health history, and then make better decisions.

news.title: ‘It feels like a medical miracle’: How did a single QR code coupon cut my $618 Walgreens prescription to $15?
news.summary: “This medication was generic,” she says. “It was a generic pill.” “I’m glad I took it,’ she says, “because it was generic.’

news.title: Micron is about to be more profitable than any U.S. company except Nvidia and Google
news.summary: Big Tech companies are willing to pay astronomical prices for AI memory components, helping spark a dramatic turnaround in Micron’s finances. Micron is a memory supplier to Apple, Google, Facebook and other tech giants.

news.title: Forget the ‘Sell America’ trade: Why U.S. markets keep proving the naysayers wrong
news.summary: The dollar remains the undisputed global reserve currency. Foreign investors are still pouring money into U.S. assets, and the dollar is still the most popular currency.

news.title: ‘She is retired’: Do I dip into my 401(k) to pay my mother’s $30,000 credit-card debt?
news.summary: “I want her to live on her Social Security instead of using it to pay off her credit-card debt,” she says.
